zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Baker於1502年出生在英國諾福爾可爾頓時，她的父親William Baker20歲，母親Margery Hawis20歲。她於1531年11月6日與Capt. Thomas S. Warner在英國諾福爾可爾頓結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1572年在英國諾福爾可享年70歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Baker于1502年出生在英国諾福爾可爾頓时，她的父亲William Baker20岁，母亲Margery Hawis20岁。她于1531年11月6日与Capt. Thomas S. Warner在英国諾福爾可爾頓结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1572年在英国諾福爾可享年70岁。

nameMeanings.name: Baker
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name, from Middle English bakere, Old English bæcere, a derivative of bacan ‘to bake’. It may have been used for someone whose special task in the kitchen of a great house or castle was the baking of bread, but since most humbler households did their own baking in the Middle Ages, it may also have referred to the owner of a communal oven used by the whole village. The right to be in charge of this and exact money or loaves in return for its use was in many parts of the country a hereditary feudal privilege. Compare Miller . Less often the surname may have been acquired by someone noted for baking particularly fine bread or by a baker of pottery or bricks.

Americanized form (translation into English) of surnames meaning ‘baker’, for example Dutch Bakker , German Becker and Beck , French Boulanger and Bélanger (see Belanger ), Czech Pekař, Slovak Pekár, and Croatian Pekar .

History: Baker was established as an early immigrant surname in Puritan New England. Among others, two men called Remember Baker (father and son) lived at Woodbury, CT, in the early 17th century, and an Alexander Baker arrived in Boston, MA, in 1635.

variants.name: Miller
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for a miller. The standard modern vocabulary word represents the northern Middle English term miller, an agent derivative of mille ‘mill’, reinforced by Old Norse mylnari (see Milner ). In southern, western, and central England Millward (literally, ‘mill keeper’) was the usual term. In North America, the surname Miller has absorbed many cognate surnames from other languages, for example German Müller (see Mueller ), Dutch Mulder and Molenaar , French Meunier , Italian Molinaro , Spanish Molinero , Hungarian Molnár (see Molnar ), Slovenian, Croatian, and Serbian Mlinar , Polish Młynarz or Młynarczyk (see Mlynarczyk ). Miller (including in the senses below) is the seventh most frequent surname in the US.

South German, Swiss German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of Müller ‘miller’ (see Mueller ) and, in North America, also an altered form of this. This form of the surname is also found in other European countries, notably in Poland, Denmark, France (mainly Alsace and Lorraine), and Czechia; compare 3 below.
Americanized form of Polish, Czech, Croatian, Serbian, and Slovenian   Miler   ‘miller’, a surname of German origin.

variants.name: Baxter
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English baxter ‘baker’ (from Old English bæcestre ‘baker’, earlier ‘female baker’, the feminine equivalent of bæcere). Compare Baker .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Baker was born in 1502, in Helhoughton, Norfolk, England, her father, William Baker, was 20 and her mother, Margery Hawis, was 20. She married Capt. Thomas S. Warner on 6 November 1531, in Helhoughton, Norfolk, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 6 daughters. She died in 1572, in Norfolk, England, at the age of 70.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Baker est née en 1502, à Helhoughton, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, William Baker, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Margery Hawis, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Capt. Thomas S. Warner le 6 novembre 1531, à Helhoughton, Norfolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée en 1572, à Norfolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 70 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Baker 1502 in Helhoughton, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Baker 20 und ihre Mutter Margery Hawis 20. Sie hat am 6. November 1531 in Helhoughton, Norfolk, England Capt. Thomas S. Warner geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb 1572 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Norfolk, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Baker è nata il nel 1502 nel Helhoughton, Norfolk, England, sua padre, William Baker, aveva 20 anni e sua madre, Margery Hawis, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Capt. Thomas S. Warner il 6 novembre 1531, nel Helhoughton, Norfolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 6 figlie. Morì nel 1572, nel Norfolk, England, all'età di 70 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Bakerが1502年にイギリスノーフォルクヘルhオウトンで生まれた時，父William Bakerは20歳，母Margery Hawisは20歳であった。1531年11月6日にイギリスノーフォルクヘルhオウトンでCapt. Thomas S. Warnerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1572年にイギリスノーフォルクで70歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Baker(이)가 1502년 영국 노르폴크 헬하그h턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Baker(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margery Hawis(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1531년 11월 6일 영국 노르폴크 헬하그h턴에서 Capt. Thomas S. Warner(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1572년 영국 노르폴크에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Baker nasceu em 1502, em Helhoughton, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Baker, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Margery Hawis, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Capt. Thomas S. Warner em 6 de novembro de 1531, em Helhoughton, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 6 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1572, em Norfolk, Inglaterra, com 70 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Baker родилась в 1502 г. в местности хелхоугтон, норфолк, Англия, её отцу по имени William Baker было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Margery Hawis, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Capt. Thomas S. Warner 6 ноября 1531 г. в местности хелхоугтон, норфолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Она умерла в 1572 г. в местности норфолк, Англия в возрасте 70 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Baker nació en 1502, en Helhoughton, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, William Baker, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Margery Hawis, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Capt. Thomas S. Warner el 6 de noviembre de 1531, en Helhoughton, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió en 1572, en Norfolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 70 años.
